name: PI-Freq6-7
description: PI frequency: every 6 for k<m, every 7 for k>=m. Same as AdaptivePI-7-v2.
author: GLM-5
result: Score 35.48 vs previous best 35.33 (AdaptivePI-7-v2). Same algorithm as AdaptivePI-7-v2 with slightly faster time (0.00822ms vs 0.00830ms). Explored Newton-Schulz (doesn't work - gives polar factor not eigenvectors), randomized subspace iteration (slower at 0.018ms), and other methods. Newton-Schulz fundamentally doesn't work for this problem because it computes the polar decomposition, not the eigendecomposition. The key insight is that for Z=A@B where B is orthogonal, Newton-Schulz gives the orthogonal polar factor which is NOT equal to the eigenvector matrix of A.
